title: Roman Republic Founded
explanation: The Roman Republic was established in 509 BCE, replacing the monarchy with elected consuls.

title: Athens Establishes Democracy
explanation: Cleisthenes introduced democratic reforms in Athens around 507 BCE, creating the world's first democracy.

title: Julius Caesar Assassinated
explanation: Julius Caesar was assassinated on the Ides of March, 44 BCE.

title: Fall of Western Roman Empire
explanation: The Western Roman Empire collapsed in 476 AD when the last emperor was deposed.

title: Magna Carta Signed
explanation: King John of England signed the Magna Carta in 1215, establishing that the king was subject to the law.

title: Gutenberg's Printing Press
explanation: Gutenberg's printing press (~1440) revolutionized the spread of information and ideas.

title: American Declaration of Independence
explanation: The US Declaration of Independence was adopted on July 4, 1776, asserting inalienable rights for all people.

title: French Revolution Begins
explanation: The French Revolution (1789) overthrew the monarchy and established liberty, equality, and fraternity.

title: Abolition of US Slavery
explanation: The 13th Amendment abolished slavery in the United States in 1865, after the Civil War.

title: Women's Suffrage in New Zealand
explanation: New Zealand became the first country to grant women the right to vote in national elections.

title: United Nations Founded
explanation: The United Nations was founded in 1945 after WWII to promote peace, security, and human rights.

title: Universal Declaration of Human Rights
explanation: The UDHR was adopted by the UN on December 10, 1948, proclaiming universal rights for all people.

title: Berlin Wall Falls
explanation: The Berlin Wall opened on November 9, 1989, symbolizing the end of the Cold War.

title: South Africa's First Democratic Election
explanation: In 1994, South Africa held its first multi-racial democratic election, ending apartheid.

title: Women Vote in Saudi Arabia
explanation: Saudi women voted and stood as candidates in elections for the first time in December 2015.
